title: Notation
description: Whispering Cave, ch.3. A symbol carved into the wall, in a notation no one in the party recognizes. The Mage reads it anyway.

steps.text: Further in, the wall had been carved. Not vandalized — *carved.* A line of symbols, evenly spaced, in a notation that wasn't quite runes and wasn't quite letters and didn't belong to any system the cave had any business knowing.

mage.text: The lead was already three feet from the wall, journal open, the pencil moving the way pencils move when they have already understood a thing and are catching up to it.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: It's a sequence. Whoever wrote it knew about the cave before the cave knew about itself.

default.text: The Mage was already at the wall. The Mage had been at the wall for some time, in fact — the party hadn't seen him peel off from the formation, but here he was, journal out, eyes moving left to right and then right to left and then left to right again, the way you read a thing that doesn't agree with itself.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: It's not a language. It's a notation. There's a difference.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Languages tell you what something *means.* Notations tell you what something *is.* This one says: the cave was measured. Recently. By someone who already knew the answer.

default.text: Nobody quite knew what to do with that, including the Mage. He wrote it down anyway. The Mage was, the party would learn, the kind of person who would rather have the wrong question written correctly than the right question forgotten.
